title: 'Tragic loss': 12 Indians among 13 killed in Qatar gas plant explosion
content: A tragic industrial accident at Qatar's Ras Laffan gas facility has claimed at least 13 lives, including Indian nationals, and injured 66. The blast at the Barzan gas supply facility, ruled an accident, occurred Sunday night. Despite the incident, Qatar's energy minister assured that domestic needs and LNG exports remain unaffected, though repairs will take time.

title: ‘Mujhe mere bete ke paas jaane do’: Grief and horror outside Lucknow fire site
content: Tragedy struck Lucknow's Aliganj as a devastating fire ripped through a three-storey commercial complex, claiming 15 young lives, mostly students at an animation centre. Families received frantic calls as thick smoke and flames engulfed the building. Brave locals and first responders risked their lives in desperate rescue attempts, while firefighters battled the blaze. The incident has left many grieving and seeking answers.

title: Messi scripts FIFA World Cup history, becomes first player ever to...
content: Lionel Messi became the outright highest scorer in FIFA World Cup history after netting his 17th goal against Austria, surpassing Miroslav Klose. The Argentina captain also equalled the record of scoring in six consecutive World Cup matches. France star Kylian Mbappe praised Messi, calling him and Cristiano Ronaldo the best, while insisting his own focus remains on winning another World Cup.

title: US judge halts DOJ subpoena for Tim Walz over immigration probe, calls it 'harassment'
content: A federal judge has halted the Trump administration's attempt to subpoena Minnesota officials, including Governor Tim Walz, deeming the move an effort to coerce cooperation with federal immigration enforcement and retaliate against them. The judge found little evidence of criminal wrongdoing, stating the subpoenas were used for "unlawful purposes" rather than a genuine investigation.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn, makes 540 workers millionaires
content: A small-town US family has sold their Louisiana-based electrical-equipment company Fibrebond to power-management giant Eaton for $1.7 billion—and made sure their 540 full-time workers got a $240 million cut of it. Former CEO Graham Walker wrote a single clause into the deal: 15% of proceeds for the staff, none of whom held any equity. The average bonus works out to around $443,000 per worker.

title: Bangladesh eyes 24 Chinese J-10CE fighter jets in big defence push: Report
content: Bangladesh is reportedly set to acquire 24 Chinese J-10CE fighter jets, with a deal potentially finalized by August. This proposed defense purchase is part of broader discussions during Prime Minister Tarique Rahman's visit to China, aimed at deepening cooperation across defense, infrastructure, trade, and investment. The visit also includes discussions on the Teesta project and China's global initiatives, with the Prime Minister attending the Summer Davos forum.

title: Lucknow building fire: 3 arrested, SIT hunts for those behind lapses
content: In the aftermath of a tragic fire in Aliganj that led to fatalities and multiple injuries, authorities have apprehended four suspects. A First Information Report has identified six individuals involved, with a manhunt underway for the remaining two. The fire broke out in a mixed-use building containing a pet shop, library, and gaming studio.

title: Two of UK's biggest telcos roll out 'kill switch' to block stolen phones from working
content: UK mobile giants Virgin Media O2 and VodafoneThree have activated a new 'kill switch' for new phones stolen from their stores, aiming to cripple the black market for stolen devices. This move follows resistance from major manufacturers like Apple and Samsung to a universal anti-theft lock. The technology renders stolen, unsold handsets unusable remotely, a measure previously seen in the Netherlands.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for June 23, 2026
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for June 23, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'Sorry, Universe Boss': Pollard's message after breaking Gayle's T20 record
content: Kieron Pollard has etched his name in T20 history, surpassing Chris Gayle to become the format's leading run-scorer during Major League Cricket 2026.  The West Indian veteran achieved this milestone with an unbeaten century, accumulating 14,582 runs. Pollard highlighted the unique challenge of his middle-order role, expressing pride in his generation's embrace of franchise cricket, which has now become a global phenomenon.

title: Lucknow fire tragedy: 15 students killed; UP CM constitutes SIT, Rajnath visits site
content: A devastating fire swept through a coaching-cum-gaming zone in Lucknow's Aliganj area on Monday afternoon, claiming the lives of at least 12 young students. Fourteen fire tenders battled the blaze that erupted around 3 pm. While authorities are investigating the exact origin, the incident has sent shockwaves through the city, with many others reportedly injured in the tragedy.

title: Why California's Billionaire's Tax is 'spooking' its millionaires now
content: California’s proposed "billionaire tax" is prompting millionaires to plan their exits. A New York Post report reveals fears that the levy could eventually target smaller fortunes. Concerns escalated after proposal author Emmanuel Saez suggested the tax might become permanent, while a business memo warned it could eventually impact the middle class.

title: 15 dead in Lucknow fire: Building was residential on paper; 16 officials under scanner
content: A deadly Aliganj fire investigation reveals the building was approved for residential use but later converted to a commercial complex. Officials are probing lapses by 16 individuals, including former employees, for alleged negligence in monitoring and approvals. The Lucknow Municipal Corporation had been collecting commercial property tax since 2022, indicating awareness of its use. The probe aims to uncover administrative negligence and regulatory violations contributing to the tragedy.

title: Panthers, Chargers eye Eagles' $21M star as NFL trade market sees major reset
content: Jalen Carter has emerged as a major name in NFL trade rumors after Jeffery Simmons signed a massive $105 million extension that could reshape the market for elite defensive tackles. With Carter still playing on his four-year, $21.8 million rookie contract, speculation is growing around whether the Philadelphia Eagles will pay up or explore trade options. The Carolina Panthers and Los Angeles Chargers have surfaced as possible landing spots.

title: Geno Smith faces explosive allegations as ex's claims spark NFL-wide controversy
content: The New York Jets’ popular quarterback Geno Smith seemingly has found himself in major trouble after a woman has accused him of domestic violence and neglecting their child in the last few hours even though he has not reacted to the drama yet and remains silent. This has sparked shockwaves across the league even though the star player has not confirmed anything yet.

title: Panthers target Samuel Ersson as Bobrovsky's Florida exit nears reality
content: The Florida Panthers are exploring a trade for Toronto Maple Leafs goalie Samuel Ersson as the likelihood of re-signing Sergei Bobrovsky continues to fade. With both Bobrovsky and Daniil Tarasov set to hit unrestricted free agency, Florida is left without a goalie under contract. Ersson, 26, had a tough season with Philadelphia but could arrive cheaply as a buy-low candidate. NHL insider Elliotte Friedman flagged the potential interest, with Toronto unlikely to keep Ersson given their existing goaltending depth.

title: The new missile order: How India is changing the way it builds its precision arsenal
content: Missiles are no longer niche assets reserved for select targets. They are increasingly becoming consumable instruments of warfare, used repeatedly to suppress air defences, destroy logistics hubs, disrupt command networks and shape battlefield conditions. The challenge, therefore, is no longer limited to developing sophisticated missiles. A production system dependent on a single dominant manufacturer can struggle to meet those demands.

title: Heatwave tightens grip on Europe: Over 20 weather-linked deaths in France & Germany
content: Europe is battling a severe heatwave, with red alerts issued across multiple nations. France is particularly hard-hit, facing record temperatures and tragic heat-related deaths, including young children. Schools are closing, and transport is disrupted as the continent grapples with extreme conditions. Scientists link this intense heat to accelerating climate change, warning of more frequent and severe heatwaves globally.

title: India vs England T20Is: Coles gets maiden call-up in 17-member squad
content: England have named a 17-man squad for the five-match T20I series against India, handing all-rounder James Coles his maiden call-up. Harry Brook will captain the side, with Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood returning. Injuries rule out Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton. The series begins on July 1, with Jos Buttler and Jofra Archer among the key names.

title: Rupee falls 30 paise to 94.63 against Dollar amid Middle East uncertainty
content: The Indian rupee weakened by 30 paise to 94.63 against the US dollar on Monday, pressured by a strong dollar and Middle East tensions. Despite supportive inflows and falling crude oil prices, importer demand and foreign institutional investors turning net sellers weighed on the currency. While equity markets saw gains, core sector growth slowed, and the RBI actively intervened in the forex market.

title: 'Don't try to change it': Sooryavanshi warned by 81-year-old cricket legend
content: Cricket legend Clive Lloyd has lauded teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, believing he has the makings of a future star. Lloyd, who faced formidable pace attacks, suggested Sooryavanshi's skills would have been a significant challenge. He also urged for the youngster's natural playing style to be preserved. This praise follows Sooryavanshi's record-breaking 11-ball fifty for India A, fueling anticipation for his potential international debut in Ireland.

title: In Pune Municipal Corporation, opposition wears helmets citing 'threat' from BJP
content: Pune Municipal Corporation proceedings were disrupted by opposition corporators protesting alleged BJP aggression with construction helmets and black armbands. This follows a chaotic previous meeting. Meanwhile, Uddhav Thackeray indicated he would soon address his party's recent setback, with six Lok Sabha MPs defecting to Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena. Shinde welcomed the MPs, calling it a continuation of his movement to preserve Balasaheb Thackeray's ideology.

title: Saints Veteran Cameron Jordan returns to NFL Top 100 ahead of Final Run
content: Cameron Jordan and Quenton Nelson returned to the NFL Top 100 after multi-year absences. Jordan’s selection comes days after the Saints veteran hinted that 2026 could be his final NFL season, while Nelson earned recognition after another dominant year anchoring the Colts’ offensive line.

title: Tesla employee's 54-hour workweek sparks debate over work-life balance
content: A Tesla worker's recent Instagram reveal of a grueling 54-hour work week, with obligations extending into weekends, has ignited significant online discourse. Many users are now pondering the balance between personal life and work commitments within the tech industry, questioning whether the strenuous hours typical of Silicon Valley jobs come at the cost of mental and physical well-being.

title: Lucknow fire: Youngsters trapped, leap from first floor as rescuers battle flames
content: Tragedy struck Lucknow's Aliganj as a devastating fire engulfed a three-floor commercial building, claiming at least 15 lives, predominantly students at an animation coaching center. Thick smoke and flames turned the premises into a death trap, prompting desperate escape attempts and a massive rescue operation. Officials confirmed the fatalities, with many injured. The incident has sparked widespread grief and calls for improved fire safety.

title: Rattled by Iran attacks, UAE looks to buy India's BrahMos & Akashteer: Report
content: India is in advanced discussions with the UAE for a significant defence deal, potentially including the sale of BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles and Akashteer air defence systems. This move signifies deepening strategic ties and India's growing prowess as a defence exporter. The UAE's interest stems from a desire to bolster its military capabilities amidst regional security concerns, marking a potential major win for India's indigenous defence industry.

title: Karnataka: 2 boys tied to school grill, beaten with slippers after cricket ball hits woman
content: Two boys, aged 14 and 12, were allegedly tied to school windows and beaten with slippers and hands in Bagalkot, Karnataka, after a cricket ball they were playing with accidentally hit a woman. Three individuals have been apprehended by police in connection with the incident, which occurred on Sunday. The injured children are currently receiving medical attention.

title: Aamir Khan reacts to wedding frenzy with Gauri Spratt, wonders how date leaked
content: Aamir Khan is all set to begin a new chapter in his life with partner Gauri Spratt. While the actor recently confirmed that the two will tie the knot on July 5, he has now opened up about the overwhelming interest surrounding the wedding, the leak of the wedding date, and the happiness Gauri has brought into his life.

title: Property inherited from parents: Check if you need to pay taxes on it
content: Inheriting property in India comes without inheritance tax, but beware of future taxation responsibilities. Local municipal bodies will impose an annual property tax. If you choose to rent out your inherited estate, remember that the rental income is taxable, albeit with possible deductions available. Should you sell the property, capital gains tax will apply, calculated on the original owner's acquisition cost, with distinct rates for both short-term and long-term profits.

title: 'Shinde has undergone Caesarean, gave birth to 6 traitors': Raut as UBT rebels join Shiv Sena
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut has sharply criticized Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde, accusing him of "giving birth to traitors" after six rebel Lok Sabha MPs joined Shinde's faction. Raut's remarks, including a reference to a "Cesarean surgery," came as Shinde declared "Operation Tiger complete" following the induction of the MPs. This marks another significant split within the Uddhav Thackeray camp.

title: Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson nightclub controversy: ECB delivers final verdict
content: England stars Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have been cleared of any wrongdoing following a nightclub incident after their win against New Zealand. While they received warnings and missed the second Test for breaching curfew, the Cricket Regulator found insufficient evidence for disciplinary action. Both players are expected to return for the series decider, shifting focus back to the 1-1 tied cricket series.

title: Watch: Australia players recreate Dhoni-Yuvraj's iconic bike celebration
content: Australia completed a 3-0 T20I series sweep over Bangladesh with a seven-wicket win, powered by Mitchell Marsh's 28-ball 60. Tim David sealed the chase and later celebrated with Matt Renshaw on a motorcycle, while Nathan Ellis also joined the festivities, recreating memories of MS Dhoni and Yuvraj Singh's iconic bike ride during their playing days.

title: Forged futures: How scammers are exploiting job aspirants
content: A disturbing surge in recruitment scams is preying on job seekers' dreams of government careers. Criminals are using forged documents, fake online exams, and impersonations of high-ranking officials like IAS and IPS officers to swindle aspirants. These elaborate frauds, often involving significant financial and emotional investment, highlight a growing trend of sophisticated cybercrime targeting hopeful individuals across India.

title: European mom adopts Indian parenting habits; says they transformed family life
content: A European mother of four, Ksenia Kala, shares how embracing Indian parenting traditions has enriched her family life. From co-sleeping and daily oil massages for babies to thoughtfully choosing Indian names and dressing up in sarees for birthdays, these practices have become cherished parts of her family's story. Kala emphasizes that good parenting ideas can be found anywhere, proving that cultural exchange can lead to beautiful family connections.

title: World Cup history! For the first time in the 21st century, Iran become...
content: Iran's veteran squad held Belgium to a goalless draw in a gritty World Cup encounter. Despite Belgium's dominance and numerous shots, Iran's solid defense, spearheaded by goalkeeper Beiranvand, proved impenetrable. A red card for Belgium's Ngoy in the second half further complicated their attacking efforts, leaving Group G wide open with all teams still in contention.

title: Father breaks down as daughter misses NEET re-exam; her response wins hearts
content: A father's desperate plea outside a NEET exam center in Vidisha, Madhya Pradesh, after his daughter arrived two minutes late, has gone viral. Despite traveling 70 kilometers and facing a motorcycle puncture, they were denied entry. The heartbreaking scene of the father collapsing highlights the immense pressure and shared anxiety families experience during these crucial exams, underscoring the profound parental role in a child's resilience.

title: Freshworks founder pays ₹211 crores to own a 7.65-ground house on Boat Club Road in Chennai
content: The transaction involved the sale of a 7.65-ground prime residential property owned by the Brij Mohan Reddy family to Girish Mathrubootham, Founder and Executive Chairman of Freshworks

title: ‘Digital literacy should reach all sections of society’
content: N. Balagopal, Vice-Chairman of the P. N. Panicker Foundation, highlights the transformative potential of digital technology during the Reading Day celebration

title: Govt. extends Solicitor-General Tushar Mehta’s term by three years
content: He has been holding the post since October 2018 and has represented the Union government in several landmark cases

title: Lucknow building fire: 15 killed as fire engulfs building, three building owners arrested
content: Fire may have started in AC duct, says Minister; eight students jumped from the building to escape the blaze, say eyewitnesses; videos showed youths falling from the first floor of the building next to a roadside railing; fire department personnel break wall to gain entry

title: Trinamool rebels remove Mamata as the party chairperson
content: Abhishek Banerjee has been removed as party general secretary; former Minister Arup Roy unanimously picked as the new chairperson of the party; the decision was taken by rebel MLAs led by Leader of Opposition Ritabrata Banerjee, who claims support of 65 out of 80 MLAs.

title: Stree Shakti, Divyang Shakti schemes increased APSRTC occupancy rate, says Minister
content: Addressing the media at the Secretariat,  Ramprasad Reddy says APSRTC’s occupancy ratio rose from 69% to 92%, thanks to the two initiatives

title: Palaniswami criticises TVK regime for not ‘showing enough attention’ to procured paddy
content: He was referring to reports that around 1 lakh bags of paddy were damaged in Kumbakonam and neighbouring areas

title: 12 Indians among 13 killed in explosion at factory in Ras Laffan Industrial City in Qatar: Officials
content: The Embassy issued the following helpline numbers: +974-55647502 or +975-55384683 and email: cons.doha@mea.gov.in

title: Facial recognition cameras at airports, vital sites to be linked to data fusion centre: CISF DG
content: Facial recognition cameras installed at six major airports likely to be integrated with the National Intelligence Grid to enhance real-time monitoring and suspect identification

title: JAMCO inaugurates Centre of Excellence in Pune
content: Aircraft interiors company aims to expand India into a design hub; local sourcing of components also on the anvil

title: CBSE Class 12 students see substantial increase in marks after re-evaluation
content: After discrepancies were reported under the new OSM evaluation system, 1.6 lakh candidates went for re-evaluation. The CBSE on June 21 released the results for 1.39 lakh of them, and said the results of the rest will be released in phases soon

title: 25 industrial projects moved out of Tamil Nadu during DMK government’s rule, alleges Minister
content: She says Tamil Nadu’s youth lost employment opportunities amounting to 2.03 lakh jobs because of this migration

title: New tribunal on Mekedatu won’t affect T.N.’s interest, says Water Resources Minister
content: He says the tribunal can establish the State’s rights over the surplus water, and it is incorrect to claim the CWMA had rejected Karnataka’s Detailed Project Report on the dam

title: Faultlines surface in Punjab Congress as Manish Tewari is kept out of consultations
content: In a cryptic post on social media platform X, the senior Congress leader invoked history to argue that factionalism has been Punjab’s worst enemy

title: Bidadi agitation expands debate on development, food security and livelihoods
content: Farmer leader Badagalapura Nagendra calls this project a ‘Bengaluru-centric’ development, which has been consuming fertile agricultural land on the city’s outskirts

title: Follow protocol at government events, Speaker tells officials
content: He instructs the Chief Secretary to issue instructions to Municipal Commissioners, Commissioners of Municipal Corporations, officials of local bodies and Collectors to adhere to the warrant of precedence

title: Hundreds of farmers join ‘Bairamangala Chalo’ rally against Bidadi Township Project
content: The rally marks the first large-scale farmers protest against the project, although local protests are ongoing for over 460 days.

title: Intellectual bankruptcy, says Congress after Suvendu government renames Suhrawardy road
content: Even Jana Sangh founder was  a supporter of Dr. Hassan Suhrawardy, the first Vice-Chancellor of Calcutta University, after whom the road was named, says Congress

title: India reports 15 lakh new cancer cases annually, focus must be on prevention and early diagnosis, says Anupriya Patel
content: The real challenge before us today is not just in providing affordable and quality treatment, but in preventing cancer and detecting it at an early stage, Union Minister of State for Health said

title: No free diagnostic services for women with more than three children: Assam hospital
content: Assembly Speaker Ranjeet Kumar Dass, who is president of a First Referral Unit in Barpeta district, says he has advised State Health Minister Ashok Singhal to implement this system in all government healthcare facilities across the State

title: Tiruvallur ammonia leak: Death toll rises to eight at seafood processing unit as six more people succumb
content: The Health department continues to closely monitor the situation and ensure the availability of appropriate medical care for all affected persons; it is coordinating with the district authorities and healthcare institutions to facilitate timely treatment, surveillance and response activities

title: Six MPs announce joining Shiv Sena under Eknath Shinde’s leadership
content: ‘We have completed the procedure in legal, constitutional and parliamentary framework; we do foolproof operations’, says Shinde; Aaditya Thackeray alleges the aim is to change Constitution by poaching legislators

title: 30 arrested for attempting irregularities in NEET-UG re-exam in Bihar
content: While 18 people working in the exam centres were arrested for violating the biometric verification norms, the majority of the others were impersonators who tried to write the exam in place of the genuine candidates

title: Three injured in clash during highway blockade in Manipur
content: Kuki civil society organisations had called the shutdown on National Highway 37 on June 22, 2026, to protest “selective security operations” allegedly targeting villages inhabited by the Kuki-Zo people

title: Farm outfits, trade unions write to PM Modi against FTA with U.S.
content: In a letter, they have sought a comprehensive Parliamentary and public review of all FTAs and full transparency in trade negotiations

title: ASI transfers ‘Rakhigarhi’ skeletons to Anthropological Survey of India for further research
content: The transfer, carried out under a recently signed MoU between the two institutions, is expected to significantly advance multidisciplinary research into one of the most important urban centres of the Indus-Saraswati Civilization, says AnSI Director

title: Private universities accused of trying to enhance engineering seats in violation of Government Order
content: They allegedly tried to do so during the transition from the Siddaramaiah-led Cabinet to the one headed by D.K. Shivakumar

title: NSA Doval meets counterparts from BRICS grouping, discusses West Asia and India-China ties
content: MEA says Doval and Iran’s Nezamipour reviewed the situation in West Asia and discussed cooperation under the BRICS platform and India-Iran bilateral ties. On meeting with China’s Wang, it says the two sides noted progress towards gradual normalisation of ties

title: Watch: U.K. PM Keir Starmer resigns: What went wrong?
content: In this episode, we look at the resignation of U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer amid mounting political pressure, the deadly ammonia gas leak at a seafood processing unit in Tamil Nadu’s Tiruvallur district, and  more.

title: Madhya Pradesh Uniform Code Draft Likely Next Month, Issues Remain
content: The committee indicated that the much-awaited UCC draft is likely to be ready by the first week of July, if not by the end of June.

title: $300-Billion Fund Sits At Heart Of US-Iran Deal: Who Pays, What To Know
content: The memorandum of understanding says Washington "undertakes with regional partners to develop a definitive mutually agreed plan with at least $300 billion for the reconstruction and economic development" of Iran.

title: "Months, Not Years": Five Eyes Alliance's Big Warning On AI Preparedness
content: The warning came in a joint statement from the Five Eyes Intelligence Oversight and Review Council (FIORC), a grouping of intelligence and security entities from Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the United Kingdom and the United States.

title: Mahrang Baloch, Prominent Baloch Activist, Sent To Life Imprisonment By Pakistan
content: Currently in custody, Mahrang Balcoh has been for years a vocal critic of the policies of the Pakistani government toward the Baloch People.

title: Approved As Residence, Used For Business: Inside Lucknow Fire That Killed 15
content: Officials said the building on Usha Mehta Marg in north Lucknow, where the fire broke out on a busy Monday afternoon, had originally been sanctioned as a residential property.

title: Pak Marriage Proposal, Name Change: Jaipur Woman Arrested Over Jaish Links
content: A preliminary examination of her mobile phone revealed two SIM cards and a Facebook account containing what officials described as objectionable content, including links to foreign profiles.

title: A Japanese AI System Reportedly Beat Claude 5 On Certain Benchmarks
content: Japanese AI company Sakana has launched an AI system called Fugu that reportedly beats Anthropic's Claude 5 on certain benchmarks.

title: 2 Children Found Dead In Car In France As Europe Sizzles Under Heatwave
content: The children, aged two and four, were found in their family car in a residential parking lot in Carpentras, where investigators said they believed the heatwave was the most likely reason for the deaths.

title: Lucknow Coaching Centre Fire: 4 Arrested, 4 Officials Suspended
content: Lucknow Coaching Centre Fire Updates: The fire, which raged for over an hour, was eventually brought under control, after which authorities recovered at least 15 bodies from the site.

title: Damage To Buildings Estimated At $1.38 Billion In Latest Israel-Lebanon War
content: Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East conflict on March 2 by launching rocket fire at Israel in support of its backer Iran.

title: Iran Says Strait Of Hormuz Closed, Tracking Data Shows Ships Crossing
content: As of 1530 GMT on Monday (late afternoon local time), the Kpler maritime tracking platform had already recorded 26 transits by commodity ships.

title: US To Ensure Any Unfrozen Iranian Funds Won't Finance Terrorism: JD Vance
content: Iran has been subject to asset freezes and sweeping sanctions by the United States and other Western countries since its 1979 Islamic revolution that toppled the US-backed Shah, Mohammad Reza Pahlavi.

title: Western Ghats In Three States To Be Declared Eco-Sensitive Areas: Sources
content: The initial proposal was to bring six states under its ambit -- Gujarat, Goa, Maharashtra, Kerala, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu. But Kerala and Karnataka are not on board with the plan.

title: Analysis: 7 PMs In 10 Years - Understanding Britain's Political Crisis
content: A large majority in Parliament often suggests political stability. But Britain's recent experience shows that numbers alone do not guarantee strong public support.

title: Smart Lights May Turn Delhi Roads Into 'Musical' Displays
content: Officials said the programmable LED network could allow authorities to create brief coordinated lighting displays across entire stretches of roads during occasions such as Diwali, Independence Day, Republic Day and an Indian cricket World Cup win.

title: Anna Hazare To Launch Fast If Amendments To RTI Rules Not Revoked
content: Activist Anna Hazare has threatened to launch an indefinite hunger strike from July 5 at Ralegan Siddhi village in Ahilyanagar district if the Maharashtra government does not immediately withdraw the "illegal" amendments made to the Right to Information Rules.

title: 12 Indians Killed In Qatar Gas Plant Explosion, Says Embassy
content: The blast happened as workers were restarting operations, which were halted after an Iranian attack in March.

title: Explained: How Next UK Prime Minister Will Be Chosen As Keir Starmer Resigns
content: Any candidate wishing to replace Starmer would need to secure the support of 20 per cent of Labour members of parliament.

title: Day Before Wedding, Bride-To-Be, Parents Die By Suicide In Karnataka
content: Rakshitha's wedding had been planned for the day after the incident. However, the family was reportedly under severe emotional distress in the days leading up to the event.

title: The skeleton lake found in 1942 still holds an unsolved secret
content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More
